task_completion.description: Verify that the dwanko_run.tnz scene is loaded in OpenToonz.

task_completion.description: Verify that the output path and format are correctly set.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog is visible, with 'Save In' set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/dailies/' (or equivalent) and the file format dropdown set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the render frame range is set to 5 through 15.

task_completion.description: Verify that the render resolution is set to 960x540.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings or Camera Settings dialog shows the Width set to 960 and Height set to 540.

task_completion.description: Verify that the sequence has been rendered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A render progress window completing the render, or a file manager showing the rendered PNG files (frames 5 to 15) in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/dailies/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to configure and render the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with OpenToonz menus, dialogs, and buttons rather than command-line tools.

integrity.description: The agent initiated a genuine render process.
integrity.visual_evidence: A render progress bar or OpenToonz rendering window is visible, proving the files were generated by the software.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing ffmpeg, Python scripts, or file manipulation commands used to fake the output.
